tex:
(a) 40 football players vote on the colour of new shirts.

The results for red, yellow and green are shown in the bar chart.

(i) (b) Twice as many football players vote blue than vote orange.

Complete the bar chart.

(c) Write down the mode.

(d) 40 hockey players vote on the colour of new shirts.

The table shows the results.

(i) (e) Complete the pie chart.

(f) Work out the percentage of hockey players who vote grey.

(a) Here is part of the timetable for buses from the station to the city centre.

All buses take the same time to travel from the station to the city centre.

(i) (b) Complete the timetable.

(c) Beth walks 4 km from her home to the station at a speed of 6 km/h.

She wants to travel on the 09 24 bus.

Work out the latest time she can leave her home.

(d) 45 seats on the bus are occupied.
This is $\dfrac{3}{5}$ of the total number of seats on the bus.

Work out the total number of seats on the bus.

(e) Beth buys 2.4 kg of onions costing \$1.25 per kilogram and 4.5 kg of potatoes.
The total cost is \$11.64.

Find the cost of 1 kg of potatoes.

(f) (i) (g) One day 140 people enter a shop.
The ratio adults : children $= 3 : 2$.

Find the number of adults who enter the shop.

(h) The price of a television in this shop is \$624.
37.5\% of this price is profit.

Calculate the profit on this television.

(i) The price of a phone in this shop is \$420.
This price increases by 12\%.

Calculate the new price.

(f) Bo has a green bag and a blue bag.
Each bag contains some marbles.

The green bag has $x$ marbles.
There are 5 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.

Bo now adds 6 marbles to each bag.
There are now 4 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.

Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.

$x = $

(a) Li spins a fair 6-sided spinner numbered 1 to 6.

(i) (b) On the probability scale, draw an arrow ($\downarrow$) to show the probability that the spinner lands on the number 2.

(c) Find the probability that the spinner lands on a prime number.

(d) Find the probability that the spinner lands on the number 7.

(e) A bag contains 3 red balls and 12 green balls.
Li picks a ball at random.

Find the probability that it is a green ball.
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

(f) Li spins two fair 4-sided spinners, each numbered 1 to 4.
The two numbers are multiplied to give the score.

Find the probability that the score is

(i) (g) an even number

(h) an integer

(i) at least 10.

(j) A bag contains red discs and blue discs.
The probability that a disc picked at random is red is $\dfrac{1}{5}$.

Li picks a disc at random, notes its colour and then replaces it in the bag.
She then picks another disc at random.

(i) (k) Complete the tree diagram.

(l) Work out the probability that both of the discs she picks are blue.
